channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Montreal author Chanel Sutherland refutes AI claims over Commonwealth Prize-winning story
item.description: Montreal-based author Chanel Sutherland has faced controversy after her Commonwealth Prize-winning short story, Descend, was flagged by an AI detection service as predominantly AI-generated. Sutherlan...

item.title: ICC confirms Duterte should remain in detention due to risk of fleeing
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has decided to uphold the detention of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte, citing concerns that he presents a higher risk of absconding if released. Thi...

item.title: Senators’ Sensitivity and Public Reactions in the Philippine Senate
item.description: The article discusses the phenomenon of emotionally sensitive senators, labeled as the 'snowflake squad,' in the Philippine Senate. The term 'snowflake' refers to individuals who are overly sensitive ...

item.title: House Prosecutors Consider Hostile Witnesses in VP Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial
item.description: The House prosecution panel is currently reviewing the potential use of hostile witnesses in the upcoming Senate impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte. According to Rep. Ramon Rodrigo Gutie...

item.title: Sam Fender honoured at Ivor Novello Awards and advocates for more working-class representation in music
item.description: Sam Fender, the singer-songwriter from North Shields, has called on the music industry to make greater efforts to discover and support talent from working-class backgrounds. He received the Songwriter...

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard steps down as Director of National Intelligence to care for ill husband
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, former Democratic congresswoman from Hawaii, has announced her resignation as Director of National Intelligence in Donald Trump's administration, effective 30 June. Gabbard cited her hu...

item.title: David Djumbe Escapes Kidnapping Attempt in Bunju with Help from Locals and Bodaboda Riders
item.description: On May 20, 2026, David Djumbe, personal assistant to jailed opposition leader Tundu Lissu, narrowly escaped an attempted abduction in Bunju. Djumbe had been picked up by his friend and neighbor, Allan...

item.title: Senate proposes 5% mandatory discount for underprivileged students on education and basic needs
item.description: Senate Bill No.1063, also known as the “Underprivileged Students’ Discount Act,” has been filed in the Philippine Senate to provide financial relief for students from low-income families. The proposed...

item.title: Tharwa Residents Face Loss of Local Post Office and Reduced Services
item.description: Residents of Tharwa, a small rural community south of Canberra, are set to lose their licensed post office after the Jeffrey family sold the Tharwa General Store. Australia Post has refused to issue a...

item.title: Virginia Giuffre's family expresses hope as police investigate Prince Andrew over alleged sex offences linked to Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Virginia Giuffre's family has welcomed a new police investigation into Prince Andrew, describing it as a significant development that offers them hope. The probe by Thames Valley Police centres on all...

item.title: Durban Harbour Pollution Threatens Local Sailing and Watersports
item.description: Durban’s sailing community is facing a serious challenge as ongoing sewage pollution in the harbour severely affects water quality, driving away boat owners and participants. The problem has intensifi...

item.title: Oyo State Suspends School Trips After Teacher Killed in Ogbomoso Attack
item.description: Dem bury Adesiyan Adegboye, one teacher wey dem kill for Ogbomoso after armed bandits attack three schools last Friday. Adegboye, wey dey 49 years old, dey among the teachers wey lose their life durin...

item.title: Winnipeg Fundraiser Highlights Support for Children with Juvenile Arthritis
item.description: Eleven-year-old Rosalie Leschyson from Winnipeg enjoys activities like playing the piano and reading, but her path hasn’t always been easy. Nearly five years ago, she began experiencing persistent wri...

item.title: WA Premier Faces Backlash Over Fracking Comments Amid Gas Supply Debate
item.description: Western Australian Premier Roger Cook has sparked criticism after suggesting that fracking in the Kimberley might be necessary if Woodside Energy's $30 billion Browse gas project does not proceed. Coo...

item.title: Sophia Loren On Set in Vittorio De Sica's Marriage Italian Style (1964)
item.description: The 1964 Italian film Marriage Italian Style, directed by the acclaimed Vittorio De Sica, remains one of the standout productions of Italian cinema's golden age. Starring the dynamic duo of Sophia Lor...

item.title: A Review of Grimaldi’s Cuccina at Durban Country Club
item.description: Grimaldi’s Cuccina at Durban Country Club offers a solid Italian dining experience in Stamford Hill. The restaurant has a modern and smart setting, with friendly service and an option to sit outside o...

item.title: Chot Reyes says Bol Bol's injury significantly affects TNT Tropang 5G
item.description: TNT Tropang 5G coach Chot Reyes recently acknowledged that the team is facing a major setback due to the injury of star player Bol Bol. The injury, which occurred ahead of crucial PBA matches, is expe...

item.title: Transformer Fire Causes Widespread Power Outage in Gurugram
item.description: On Thursday, Gurugram faced a significant power disruption after a fire broke out at the main transformer of the 220 KVA power station located in Sector-72. The incident caused electricity supply inte...

item.title: NPA challenges contempt conviction and arrest warrant for prosecutor in Joe Sibanyoni extortion matter
item.description: The National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) in South Africa has taken urgent legal steps to prevent the arrest of one of its prosecutors, Mkhuseli Ntaba, who was held in contempt of court for failing to ...

item.title: Nato Conducts Underground Exercises in London to Test Readiness Against Potential Russian Threat
item.description: Nato forces have conducted a large-scale exercise at Charing Cross Tube station in London, converting a disused platform into an underground command centre. The exercise, part of Operation Arcade Stri...
